data.description:
## Summary

Implement the approved 2026-07-19 Rebar Janitor remediation plan. The work restores optionality by correcting contributor guidance, clarifying the force/invariant contract, making LLM verification resilient without accepting empty outcomes, preventing plan reviews from passing with incomplete hierarchy context, and documenting safe ChatGPT workflows for connector-limited environments.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Contributor and agent guidance uses each contributor's configured real DCO identity; Joe Oakhart's bot identity appears only where an explicitly automated workflow requires it.
- [ ] Public transition guidance states that force bypasses all gates, including future gates, while ticket-system invariants such as the open-children close prohibition remain non-bypassable.
- [ ] Verification preserves flexible provider-independent parsing and self-healing, but no semantically empty result can be accepted as a valid review outcome.
- [ ] Plan review cannot produce an ordinary clean pass when the ticket hierarchy was loaded incompletely, unless the omitted context is demonstrably irrelevant.
- [ ] ChatGPT-specific guidance explains how to use Rebar safely when the GitHub repository is a read-only mirror and native clone, Gerrit, or tracker access is unavailable.
data.title: Janitor Cleanup 2026-07-19
